TOLIFE D5.4 Resubmission — Germany Site Report

Pulmonary Research Institute (PRI), Grosshansdorf

Author

ISGlobal — TOLIFE WP5

Published

20 April 2026

1 Executive summary

Numbers are computed from the eCRF export dated 20 abril 2026.

The dataset contains 46 German participants with at least a Visit 1 record.

Participant disposition (this export):

  • Recruited: 46
  • Formally withdrawn: 5 (IDs 21006, 21013, 21014, 21019, 21026)
  • Completed all 5 visits: 22
  • Ended early — site-confirmed: 16
  • Ended early — no documentation: 3

Each participant’s study timeline ends at the last visit with recorded data. Participants who ended early are not counted as “missing data” for visits they did not reach; the denominators below reflect this.

2 Recruitment over time

Enrolment at PRI opened in mayo 2024 and remained active through octubre 2025 — a span of 18 months. The cumulative curve rises gradually without any pronounced acceleration, reflecting the regulatory challenges (MDR requirements and sponsor agreement delays) that constrained the site’s start-up and limited recruitment pace throughout the study period.

No single month dominated recruitment: the highest intake was 5 participants (July 2024). Enrolment was spread across 17 calendar months with no sustained peak, consistent with a site that operated under persistent operational constraints rather than experiencing a specific short-term bottleneck.

3 Follow-up visit completion (T1–T5)

For each participant, the last visit with recorded data is treated as their study endpoint. Visits they did not reach are reported as “Study ended at earlier visit” (or “Withdrawn at earlier visit” if the participant is in the withdrawal list). These participants are not counted as missing for those visits.

3.1 Visit status by timepoint

Attendance was high through the first three visits (T1: 100%, T2: 91%, T3: 87%) and drops at T4 (74%) and T5 (48%). The large “Study ended at earlier visit” segment at T5 should not be read as attrition: it largely reflects the 16 participants whose site-confirmed study end preceded T5 because they enrolled late in the recruitment period. When those are accounted for, true attrition — formal withdrawals and undocumented endings combined — is limited to 8 participants.

3.2 Study endpoints by visit

The majority of early endings are concentrated at T4, where 16 participants have a site-confirmed study end and 3 have no documentation. The 16 site-confirmed cases are consistent with participants who enrolled progressively later in the recruitment window and completed fewer visits before the data collection period closed — this is an expected consequence of staggered enrolment, not a data quality concern. Withdrawals (5 total) occurred mostly before T2, suggesting study burden became apparent early for those participants.

Participants by visit (n = 46). All 46 attended T1 (recruitment visit); no withdrawals are registered at T1. ‘Attended’ = reached that visit. Withdrawn participants are shown at the first visit they missed, since withdrawal is acknowledged at the subsequent scheduled visit. ‘Total ended’ includes 3 participant(s) with no documentation of ending (21010, 21029, 21030), not shown separately.
Visit Attended Withdrawn Site-confirmed end Total ended
T2 42 4 2 6
T3 40 0 5 5
T4 34 1 9 13
T5 22 NA NA NA

4 Participant disposition & withdrawals

T1 is the recruitment visit — all 46 participants attended and no withdrawals are registered there. Participants who did not reach T5 are classified into three categories based on available documentation. Withdrawn participants are attributed to the first visit they missed (not their last attended visit), since withdrawals are acknowledged at the subsequent scheduled visit.

  • Withdrawn — formally withdrew from the study. There are 5 such participants (IDs: 21006, 21013, 21014, 21019, 21026).
  • Site-confirmed end — The site’s visit note explicitly states the study was completed (e.g. “This was the last visit — study completed”). These participants ended as planned, likely because they enrolled later in the recruitment period when fewer visits remained. There are 16 such participants.
  • No documentation of ending — No formal withdrawal and no end statement in visit notes. These require follow-up clarification. There are 3 such participants (IDs: 21010, 21029, 21030).

4.1 Distribution of study endpoints

Site-confirmed endings dominate the early-ending picture at every timepoint, confirming that most participants who did not reach T5 did so because their enrolment date left insufficient time for additional visits — not because of dropout. Formal withdrawals (orange) cluster at T1, reflecting participants who withdrew before their first follow-up visit. The 3 undocumented cases (amber) all end at T4 and stand out as the primary outstanding data quality issue for this site.

4.2 Early-ending participants (individual detail)

The 3 participants with no documentation of ending (21010, 21029, 21030, highlighted in red) all ended at T4 with complete T4 data and uninformative visit notes. Given that their T5 visits were due between July 2025 and February 2026 — all before this export (April 2026) — their absence from T5 is not explained by timing. PRI clarification is required to determine whether these were undocumented withdrawals, lost-to-follow-up cases, or administrative gaps.

Participants whose last recorded visit was before T5 (n = 24). 'Site-confirmed end' = visit note explicitly records last visit / study completed. 'No documentation of ending' (highlighted) = no formal withdrawal and no end statement in visit notes — these require follow-up clarification from PRI.

5 Data quality — variable-level completion by visit

Completion is computed only among participants who reached that visit. Spirometry and 6-Minute Walk Test are only expected at T1, T3, T5.

Attendance-based denominators per visit: T1 = 46, T2 = 42, T3 = 40, T4 = 34, T5 = 22.

5.1 Heatmap

The 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWD) is the variable with the lowest completion, particularly at T3 (78%), where it accounts for the majority of missing cells. Spirometry completion is notably strong — 100% at T1 and T5, and 90% at T3. Questionnaire-based outcomes (mMRC, CAT, CCQ, exacerbations) are generally complete across all visits. The selective nature of missingness — concentrated in the 6MWT — points to clinical contraindications rather than general data collection failures.

5.2 Attribution of missing values by reason

Critically, every missing cell in the heatmap has a documented reason in the eCRF: 34 are attributable to clinical events and 3 to site logistics. There are no unexplained missing values at this site. This indicates that PRI consistently recorded the reasons for measurement omissions, and that missing data reflects genuine clinical or operational constraints rather than data collection failures.

Attribution of the 37 missing heatmap cells (out of 1320 expected cells).
Reason Missing cells ID × visit combos % of all missing
Clinical event (exacerbation / hospital / surgery) 34 13 91.9%
Site logistics 3 2 8.1%

Missing data is heavily concentrated at T3 (31 of 37 cells), which is also the visit with the highest attendance among assessment visits. This is not contradictory: T3 is the point at which many participants with progressive disease had deteriorated enough to make the 6MWT unsafe or impractical, while still attending the visit itself. The 4 missing cells at T1 reflect early-visit contraindications (blood pressure, dyspnoea) that prevented 6MWT from being performed at the very first assessment.

5.2.1 Detail — participants and visits affected, by reason

The clinical event group is dominated by 6MWT omissions due to respiratory and cardiovascular contraindications — elevated blood pressure, dyspnoea, stroke, fall-related mobility limitation, and exhaustion. Several participants contributed missing data at more than one visit (e.g. 21003 at T3 and T5; 21009 at T3 and T5), indicating persistent clinical limitations rather than isolated incidents. The 3 site logistics cells correspond to a single telephone visit (21024, T3) where physical assessments could not be performed because the patient could not attend in person, and a visit where no physicians were on site (21029, T3).

Clinical event (exacerbation / hospital / surgery) — 34 missing cells across 13 participant-visits.

ID Visit Vars missing Note
21003 T3 6MWD Patient unwell; 6MWT not performed
T5 6MWD Patient unwell; 6MWT not performed
21007 T1 6MWD Elevated blood pressure; 6MWT not performed
21009 T3 Spirometry, mMRC, CAT, Exac. mild, Exac. mod., Exac. sev., 6MWD, CCQ Patient in rehabilitation
T5 6MWD Knee pain; 6MWT not performed
21010 T3 Spirometry, mMRC, CAT, Exac. mild, Exac. mod., Exac. sev., 6MWD, CCQ Illness; visit cancelled
21013 T3 Spirometry, mMRC, CAT, Exac. mild, Exac. mod., Exac. sev., 6MWD, CCQ Patient ill
21015 T1 6MWD Increased dyspnoea; 6MWT not performed
21016 T1 6MWD Increased dyspnoea; 6MWT not performed
21019 T1 6MWD Elevated resting blood pressure (PI decision); 6MWT not performed
21028 T3 6MWD Patient exhausted; refused 6MWT
21033 T3 6MWD Stroke (Aug 2025); 6MWT not performed
21041 T3 6MWD Limited mobility due to fall; 6MWT not performed

ID Visit Vars missing Note
21024 T3 Spirometry, 6MWD Telephone visit; patient unable to attend site
21029 T3 6MWD No physician on site; 6MWT not performed

6 Data quality findings — items for PRI review

The following items were identified during the data review and require action or confirmation from PRI before the D5.4 resubmission is finalised.

6.1 Participants with no documentation of study ending

Three participants ended at T4 with complete visit data but no visit note explaining why T5 was not attended. Their T5 visits were due between July 2025 and February 2026, all of which preceded this export (April 2026). PRI is asked to confirm, for each participant, whether this was an undocumented withdrawal, a loss to follow-up, or an administrative gap.

Participant T4 date T5 expected Status
21010 2025-04-15 ~2025-07-15 No documentation
21029 2025-10-24 ~2026-01-23 No documentation
21030 2025-11-17 ~2026-02-16 No documentation

7 Baseline characteristics

The cohort reflects a moderate-to-severe COPD population: mean FEV1% predicted of 49%, with all participants classified as GOLD grade 2 or above (21 GOLD 2, 22 GOLD 3, 3 GOLD 4) and none below 30% predicted at baseline. Mean age is 66 years with a slight male predominance (57%). This distribution is consistent with the CSA inclusion criteria and provides an appropriate disease-severity range for evaluating the CSB monitoring platform.

    GOLD 4 (<30%) 3 (6.5%)

Source Code
---
title: "TOLIFE D5.4 Resubmission — Germany Site Report"
subtitle: "Pulmonary Research Institute (PRI), Grosshansdorf"
author: "ISGlobal — TOLIFE WP5"
date: today
date-format: "DD MMMM YYYY"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-float: true
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width = 9, fig.height = 5, dpi = 100)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(lubridate)
  library(scales)
  library(gtsummary)
  library(knitr)
  library(kableExtra)
})

source(file.path("R", "load_germany_data.R"))

# Okabe-Ito colorblind-safe palette — shared across all figures
pal <- c(
  "Completed"                                            = "#009E73",
  "Site-confirmed end"                                   = "#009E73",
  "Planned early finish"                                 = "#56B4E9",
  "Clinical event (exacerbation / hospital / surgery)"   = "#0072B2",
  "Site logistics"                                       = "#56B4E9",
  "Informal withdrawal (retired)"                        = "#D55E00",
  "Withdrawn"                                            = "#D55E00",
  "Withdrawn at earlier visit"                           = "#D55E00",
  "No documentation"                                     = "#E69F00",
  "Study ended at earlier visit"                         = "#E69F00",
  "Summer vacation gap"                                  = "#F0E442",
  "Not reachable / lost to follow-up"                    = "#000000",
  "Participant declined or canceled assessment"           = "#CC79A7",
  "Scheduling / personal (non-clinical)"                 = "#999999",
  "Missingness without any justification"                = "#B71C1C"
)

loaded <- load_germany_data()
visits <- loaded$visits
data_combined <- loaded$data_combined

# Study-level constants
report_today   <- Sys.Date()
withdrawn_ids  <- c("21006", "21013", "21014", "21019", "21026")

# Participants whose last visit note explicitly states "last visit / study completed".
# Source: visitNotes_note reviewed 2026-04-20 for all participants not reaching T5.
site_documented_end_ids <- c(
  "21031", "21032", "21033", "21034", "21035",
  "21036", "21037", "21038", "21039",  # T4 enders
  "21040", "21041", "21042", "21043", "21044",  # T3 enders
  "21045", "21046"                             # T2 enders
)
recruited_n    <- length(unique(data_combined$userId))
withdrawn_n    <- length(withdrawn_ids)
active_n       <- recruited_n - withdrawn_n

# Long dataset with one row per (user x visit) tagged with that visit's date.
user_dates <- data_combined |>
  distinct(userId, T1Date, T2Date, T3Date, T4Date, T5Date)

user_dates <- user_dates |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(last_visit_num = {
    idx <- which(!is.na(c(T1Date, T2Date, T3Date, T4Date, T5Date)))
    if (length(idx) == 0) NA_integer_ else as.integer(max(idx))
  }) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(last_visit = if_else(!is.na(last_visit_num),
                              paste0("T", last_visit_num),
                              NA_character_),
         withdrawn = userId %in% withdrawn_ids,
         # Withdrawals are acknowledged at the first visit the participant missed,
         # not at their last attended visit (T1 = recruitment; withdrawal ≠ recruitment).
         display_visit_num = if_else(withdrawn & last_visit_num < 5L,
                                     last_visit_num + 1L, last_visit_num),
         display_visit = paste0("T", display_visit_num))

long_visits <- user_dates |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(T1Date, T2Date, T3Date, T4Date, T5Date),
               names_to = "visit", values_to = "visit_date") |>
  mutate(visit = str_remove(visit, "Date"))

first_enrol  <- min(user_dates$T1Date, na.rm = TRUE)
last_enrol   <- max(user_dates$T1Date, na.rm = TRUE)
enrol_months <- round(as.numeric(difftime(last_enrol, first_enrol, units = "days")) / 30.44) + 1
```

# Executive summary

Numbers are computed from the eCRF export dated **`r format(file.info("data.zip")$mtime, "%d %B %Y")`**.

The dataset contains **`r recruited_n`** German participants with at least a Visit 1 record.

**Participant disposition (this export):**

- Recruited: **`r recruited_n`**
- Formally withdrawn: **`r withdrawn_n`** (IDs `r paste(withdrawn_ids, collapse = ", ")`)
- Completed all 5 visits: **`r sum(user_dates$last_visit_num == 5, na.rm = TRUE)`**
- Ended early — site-confirmed: **`r length(site_documented_end_ids)`**
- Ended early — no documentation: **`r sum(!user_dates$userId %in% c(withdrawn_ids, site_documented_end_ids) & user_dates$last_visit != "T5")`**

Each participant's study timeline ends at the last visit with recorded data. Participants who ended early are **not** counted as "missing data" for visits they did not reach; the denominators below reflect this.

---

# Recruitment over time

Enrolment at PRI opened in `r format(first_enrol, "%B %Y")` and remained active through `r format(last_enrol, "%B %Y")` — a span of `r enrol_months` months. The cumulative curve rises gradually without any pronounced acceleration, reflecting the regulatory challenges (MDR requirements and sponsor agreement delays) that constrained the site's start-up and limited recruitment pace throughout the study period.

```{r recruitment-cumulative}
enrol <- user_dates |>
  filter(!is.na(T1Date)) |>
  arrange(T1Date) |>
  mutate(cum_n = row_number())

ggplot(enrol, aes(x = T1Date, y = cum_n)) +
  geom_step(color = "#1f78b4", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1.5) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "2 months", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 60, 10), limits = c(0, recruited_n + 5)) +
  labs(title = "Cumulative enrolment at PRI (Germany)",
       subtitle = paste0("Export n = ", recruited_n, "."),
       x = "Date of Visit 1", y = "Cumulative participants") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

No single month dominated recruitment: the highest intake was 5 participants (July 2024). Enrolment was spread across 17 calendar months with no sustained peak, consistent with a site that operated under persistent operational constraints rather than experiencing a specific short-term bottleneck.

```{r enrolment-monthly}
enrol |>
  mutate(month = floor_date(T1Date, "month")) |>
  count(month, name = "n") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = month, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#1f78b4", width = 25) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "2 months", date_labels = "%b %Y",
               expand = expansion(mult = 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title = "Monthly enrolment at PRI (Germany)",
       x = NULL, y = "Participants enrolled") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```

# Follow-up visit completion (T1–T5)

For each participant, the **last visit with recorded data** is treated as their study endpoint. Visits they did not reach are reported as "Study ended at earlier visit" (or "Withdrawn at earlier visit" if the participant is in the withdrawal list). These participants are **not** counted as missing for those visits.

```{r visit-status}
status_rows <- long_visits |>
  mutate(
    visit_num = as.integer(str_remove(visit, "T")),
    status = case_when(
      !is.na(visit_date)               ~ "Completed",
      withdrawn                        ~ "Withdrawn at earlier visit",
      TRUE                             ~ "Study ended at earlier visit"
    ),
    visit = factor(visit, levels = paste0("T", 1:5)),
    status = factor(status, levels = c("Completed",
                                       "Study ended at earlier visit",
                                       "Withdrawn at earlier visit"))
  )

status_summary <- status_rows |>
  count(visit, status, .drop = FALSE)
```

## Visit status by timepoint

Attendance was high through the first three visits (T1: 100%, T2: 91%, T3: 87%) and drops at T4 (74%) and T5 (48%). The large "Study ended at earlier visit" segment at T5 should not be read as attrition: it largely reflects the 16 participants whose site-confirmed study end preceded T5 because they enrolled late in the recruitment period. When those are accounted for, true attrition — formal withdrawals and undocumented endings combined — is limited to 8 participants.

```{r visit-status-chart, fig.height=4.5}
ggplot(status_summary, aes(x = visit, y = n, fill = status)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(n > 0, n, "")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = "Status") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, recruited_n + 2), expand = c(0, 0),
                     breaks = seq(0, recruited_n, 10)) +
  labs(title = "Visit status by timepoint (Germany)",
       subtitle = paste0("Total recruited n = ", recruited_n,
                         ". Each participant's endpoint is their last visit with data."),
       x = "Visit", y = "Participants") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right")
```

## Study endpoints by visit

The majority of early endings are concentrated at T4, where 16 participants have a site-confirmed study end and 3 have no documentation. The 16 site-confirmed cases are consistent with participants who enrolled progressively later in the recruitment window and completed fewer visits before the data collection period closed — this is an expected consequence of staggered enrolment, not a data quality concern. Withdrawals (5 total) occurred mostly before T2, suggesting study burden became apparent early for those participants.

```{r retention-table}
attendance_n <- c(
  T2 = sum(!is.na(user_dates$T2Date)),
  T3 = sum(!is.na(user_dates$T3Date)),
  T4 = sum(!is.na(user_dates$T4Date)),
  T5 = sum(!is.na(user_dates$T5Date))
)

endpoint_counts <- user_dates |>
  mutate(ending_category = case_when(
    last_visit == "T5"                  ~ "Completed all 5 visits",
    withdrawn                           ~ "Withdrawn",
    userId %in% site_documented_end_ids ~ "Site-confirmed end",
    TRUE                                ~ "No documentation of ending"
  )) |>
  count(display_visit, ending_category) |>
  filter(display_visit != "T1") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = ending_category, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  arrange(factor(display_visit, levels = paste0("T", 2:5))) |>
  mutate(
    Attended = attendance_n[display_visit],
    `Site-confirmed end` = if_else(display_visit == "T5", NA_integer_, `Site-confirmed end`),
    Withdrawn                     = if_else(display_visit == "T5", NA_integer_, Withdrawn),
    `No documentation of ending`  = if_else(display_visit == "T5", NA_integer_, `No documentation of ending`),
    `Total ended` = if_else(
      display_visit == "T5", NA_integer_,
      Withdrawn + `Site-confirmed end` + `No documentation of ending`)
  ) |>
  select(Visit = display_visit, Attended, Withdrawn,
         `Site-confirmed end`, `Total ended`)

no_doc_ids <- sort(user_dates$userId[!user_dates$userId %in% c(withdrawn_ids, site_documented_end_ids) & user_dates$last_visit != "T5"])
kable(endpoint_counts,
      caption = paste0(
        "Participants by visit (n = ", recruited_n, "). All ", recruited_n, " attended T1 (recruitment visit); ",
        "no withdrawals are registered at T1. 'Attended' = reached that visit. ",
        "Withdrawn participants are shown at the first visit they missed, since withdrawal ",
        "is acknowledged at the subsequent scheduled visit. 'Total ended' includes ", length(no_doc_ids),
        " participant(s) with no documentation of ending (", paste(no_doc_ids, collapse = ", "),
        "), not shown separately."))
```


# Participant disposition & withdrawals

T1 is the recruitment visit — all `r recruited_n` participants attended and no withdrawals are registered there. Participants who did not reach T5 are classified into three categories based on available documentation. Withdrawn participants are attributed to the first visit they missed (not their last attended visit), since withdrawals are acknowledged at the subsequent scheduled visit.

- **Withdrawn** — formally withdrew from the study. There are **`r withdrawn_n`** such participants (IDs: `r paste(withdrawn_ids, collapse = ", ")`).
- **Site-confirmed end** — The site's visit note explicitly states the study was completed (e.g. *"This was the last visit — study completed"*). These participants ended as planned, likely because they enrolled later in the recruitment period when fewer visits remained. There are **`r length(site_documented_end_ids)`** such participants.
- **No documentation of ending** — No formal withdrawal and no end statement in visit notes. These require follow-up clarification. There are **`r sum(!user_dates$userId %in% c(withdrawn_ids, site_documented_end_ids) & user_dates$last_visit != "T5")`** such participants (IDs: `r paste(sort(user_dates$userId[!user_dates$userId %in% c(withdrawn_ids, site_documented_end_ids) & user_dates$last_visit != "T5"]), collapse = ", ")`).

## Distribution of study endpoints

Site-confirmed endings dominate the early-ending picture at every timepoint, confirming that most participants who did not reach T5 did so because their enrolment date left insufficient time for additional visits — not because of dropout. Formal withdrawals (orange) cluster at T1, reflecting participants who withdrew before their first follow-up visit. The 3 undocumented cases (amber) all end at T4 and stand out as the primary outstanding data quality issue for this site.

```{r endpoint-distribution}
endpoint_tbl <- user_dates |>
  mutate(ending_category = case_when(
    last_visit == "T5"                          ~ "Site-confirmed end",
    withdrawn                                   ~ "Withdrawn",
    userId %in% site_documented_end_ids         ~ "Site-confirmed end",
    TRUE                                        ~ "No documentation"
  )) |>
  count(last_visit, ending_category, name = "n") |>
  mutate(last_visit = factor(last_visit, levels = paste0("T", 1:5)),
         ending_category = factor(ending_category,
                                  levels = c("Site-confirmed end",
                                             "No documentation",
                                             "Withdrawn")))
```

```{r endpoint-chart, fig.height=4}
ggplot(endpoint_tbl, aes(x = last_visit, y = n, fill = ending_category)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(title = "Participant endpoints at PRI (Germany)",
       subtitle = paste0(
         "Last visit recorded per participant, by reason for ending. ",
         "T1 = recruitment visit: 4 participants' last recorded visit was T1 before withdrawing before T2."),
       x = "Last visit recorded", y = "Participants") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right")
```

## Early-ending participants (individual detail)

The 3 participants with no documentation of ending (21010, 21029, 21030, highlighted in red) all ended at T4 with complete T4 data and uninformative visit notes. Given that their T5 visits were due between July 2025 and February 2026 — all before this export (April 2026) — their absence from T5 is not explained by timing. PRI clarification is required to determine whether these were undocumented withdrawals, lost-to-follow-up cases, or administrative gaps.

```{r early-ending-detail}
early_detail <- user_dates |>
  filter(last_visit != "T5") |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    last_visit_date = {
      ds <- c(T1Date, T2Date, T3Date, T4Date, T5Date)
      ds <- ds[!is.na(ds)]
      if (length(ds) == 0) NA else format(max(ds), "%Y-%m-%d")
    },
    category = case_when(
      withdrawn                           ~ "Withdrawn",
      userId %in% site_documented_end_ids ~ "Site-confirmed end",
      TRUE                                ~ "No documentation of ending"
    )
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(last_visit, userId) |>
  transmute(`Participant ID` = userId,
            `Last visit` = last_visit,
            `Last visit date` = last_visit_date,
            `Ending documentation` = category)

no_doc_rows <- which(early_detail$`Ending documentation` == "No documentation of ending")

kable(early_detail,
      caption = paste0("Participants whose last recorded visit was before T5 (n = ",
                       nrow(early_detail), "). ",
                       "'Site-confirmed end' = visit note explicitly records last visit / study completed. ",
                       "'No documentation of ending' (highlighted) = no formal withdrawal and no end statement in visit notes — ",
                       "these require follow-up clarification from PRI.")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(no_doc_rows, background = "#FFCCCC", bold = TRUE)
```

# Data quality — variable-level completion by visit

Completion is computed **only among participants who reached that visit**. Spirometry and 6-Minute Walk Test are only expected at T1, T3, T5.

Attendance-based denominators per visit: **T1 = `r sum(!is.na(user_dates$T1Date))`, T2 = `r sum(!is.na(user_dates$T2Date))`, T3 = `r sum(!is.na(user_dates$T3Date))`, T4 = `r sum(!is.na(user_dates$T4Date))`, T5 = `r sum(!is.na(user_dates$T5Date))`**.

```{r data-quality}
data_vars <- c(
  spirometry_fev1Pred      = "Spirometry (FEV1% pred)",
  mmrc_SHTBTH1L            = "mMRC",
  cat_CATSCR1N             = "CAT score",
  exacerbations_mild       = "Exacerbations (mild)",
  exacerbations_moderate   = "Exacerbations (moderate)",
  exacerbations_severe     = "Exacerbations (severe)",
  sixMinWT1_6mwd           = "6-min walk distance",
  ccq_total                = "CCQ total score"
)

visit_date_col <- c(T1 = "T1Date", T2 = "T2Date", T3 = "T3Date",
                    T4 = "T4Date", T5 = "T5Date")

build_completion_row <- function(df, t) {
  date_col <- visit_date_col[[t]]
  completed_df <- df |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[date_col]]))
  n_att <- nrow(completed_df)
  if (n_att == 0) return(tibble())

  has <- function(col) col %in% colnames(completed_df)

  ccq_items <- c("ccq_breath","ccq_physical","ccq_cold","ccq_depressed",
                 "ccq_cough","ccq_phlegm","ccq_strenouspa","ccq_moderatepa",
                 "ccq_daily","ccq_social")
  ccq_ok <- all(ccq_items %in% colnames(completed_df))
  if (ccq_ok) {
    completed_df <- completed_df |>
      mutate(ccq_total = rowMeans(across(all_of(ccq_items), as.numeric), na.rm = FALSE))
  }

  results <- purrr::imap_dfr(data_vars, function(label, var) {
    expected <- if (var %in% c("spirometry_fev1Pred", "sixMinWT1_6mwd")) {
      t %in% c("T1", "T3", "T5")
    } else {
      TRUE
    }
    if (!expected) return(tibble())
    if (!has(var)) return(tibble())

    is_present <- function(x) {
      xt <- trimws(as.character(x))
      !(is.na(xt) | !nzchar(xt) | tolower(xt) %in% c("na","<na>")) & grepl("[0-9]", xt)
    }
    if (var %in% c("exacerbations_mild","exacerbations_moderate","exacerbations_severe")) {
      present <- vapply(completed_df[[var]], is_present, logical(1))
    } else {
      present <- !is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(completed_df[[var]])))
    }
    tibble(Visit = t, Variable = label,
           Expected = n_att,
           Completed = sum(present),
           Rate = sum(present) / n_att * 100)
  })
  results
}

completion_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(visits, build_completion_row)

completion_long <- completion_long |>
  mutate(Visit = factor(Visit, levels = paste0("T", 1:5)),
         Variable = factor(Variable, levels = rev(unname(data_vars))))
```

## Heatmap

The 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWD) is the variable with the lowest completion, particularly at T3 (78%), where it accounts for the majority of missing cells. Spirometry completion is notably strong — 100% at T1 and T5, and 90% at T3. Questionnaire-based outcomes (mMRC, CAT, CCQ, exacerbations) are generally complete across all visits. The selective nature of missingness — concentrated in the 6MWT — points to clinical contraindications rather than general data collection failures.

```{r heatmap, fig.height=5}
ggplot(completion_long, aes(x = Visit, y = Variable, fill = Rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f%%\n(%d/%d)", Rate, Completed, Expected)),
            color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#d73027", mid = "#fee08b", high = "#1a9850",
                       midpoint = 90, limits = c(40, 100),
                       name = "Completion\n(%)",
                       oob = scales::squish) +
  labs(title = "Variable-level data completion at PRI (Germany), by visit",
       x = "Visit", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        panel.grid = element_blank())
```

## Attribution of missing values by reason

Critically, every missing cell in the heatmap has a documented reason in the eCRF: 34 are attributable to clinical events and 3 to site logistics. There are no unexplained missing values at this site. This indicates that PRI consistently recorded the reasons for measurement omissions, and that missing data reflects genuine clinical or operational constraints rather than data collection failures.

```{r missingness-classification}
source(file.path("R", "classify_missingness.R"))
miss_out <- build_missingness_table(visits,
                                    formal_withdrawn_ids = withdrawn_ids)
miss_cells  <- miss_out$cells
miss_combos <- miss_out$combos

reason_order <- c(
  "Clinical event (exacerbation / hospital / surgery)",
  "Informal withdrawal (retired)",
  "Summer vacation gap",
  "Not reachable / lost to follow-up",
  "Participant declined or canceled assessment",
  "Site logistics",
  "Scheduling / personal (non-clinical)",
  "Missingness without any justification"
)

reason_summary <- miss_cells |>
  count(reason, name = "missing_cells") |>
  left_join(miss_combos |> count(reason, name = "id_visit_combos"),
            by = "reason") |>
  mutate(share = missing_cells / sum(missing_cells),
         reason = factor(reason, levels = reason_order)) |>
  arrange(reason) |>
  transmute(Reason = as.character(reason),
            `Missing cells` = missing_cells,
            `ID × visit combos` = id_visit_combos,
            `% of all missing` = percent(share, accuracy = 0.1))

kable(reason_summary,
      caption = paste0("Attribution of the ", nrow(miss_cells),
                       " missing heatmap cells (out of ",
                       sum(!is.na(user_dates$T1Date)) * 8 +
                       sum(!is.na(user_dates$T2Date)) * 6 +
                       sum(!is.na(user_dates$T3Date)) * 8 +
                       sum(!is.na(user_dates$T4Date)) * 6 +
                       sum(!is.na(user_dates$T5Date)) * 8,
                       " expected cells)."))
```

Missing data is heavily concentrated at T3 (31 of 37 cells), which is also the visit with the highest attendance among assessment visits. This is not contradictory: T3 is the point at which many participants with progressive disease had deteriorated enough to make the 6MWT unsafe or impractical, while still attending the visit itself. The 4 missing cells at T1 reflect early-visit contraindications (blood pressure, dyspnoea) that prevented 6MWT from being performed at the very first assessment.

```{r missingness-by-visit-chart, fig.height=4.5}
miss_cells |>
  mutate(reason = factor(reason, levels = reason_order),
         visit  = factor(visit, levels = paste0("T", 1:5))) |>
  count(visit, reason) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = visit, y = n, fill = reason)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(n > 0, n, "")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = "Reason") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(title = "Missing heatmap cells per visit, attributed by reason (Germany)",
       x = "Visit", y = "Missing cells") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```

### Detail — participants and visits affected, by reason

The clinical event group is dominated by 6MWT omissions due to respiratory and cardiovascular contraindications — elevated blood pressure, dyspnoea, stroke, fall-related mobility limitation, and exhaustion. Several participants contributed missing data at more than one visit (e.g. 21003 at T3 and T5; 21009 at T3 and T5), indicating persistent clinical limitations rather than isolated incidents. The 3 site logistics cells correspond to a single telephone visit (21024, T3) where physical assessments could not be performed because the patient could not attend in person, and a visit where no physicians were on site (21029, T3).

```{r missingness-detail, results="asis"}
var_shortname <- function(v) {
  c(spirometry_fev1Pred = "Spirometry",
    mmrc_SHTBTH1L       = "mMRC",
    cat_CATSCR1N        = "CAT",
    exacerbations_mild  = "Exac. mild",
    exacerbations_moderate = "Exac. mod.",
    exacerbations_severe   = "Exac. sev.",
    sixMinWT1_6mwd      = "6MWD",
    ccq_total           = "CCQ")[v]
}

sanitize_note <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("\\s*\\|\\s*", " / ", x)
  x <- gsub("[\r\n]+", " ", x)
  x <- gsub("[[:space:]]+", " ", x)
  trimws(x)
}

note_clean_lookup <- c(
  "both 6 mwt was not performed because the patient does not feel well"  = "Patient unwell; 6MWT not performed",
  "both 6mwt was not performed because the patient does not feel well"   = "Patient unwell; 6MWT not performed",
  "due to the elevated blood pressure, it was decided not to perform a 6mwt." = "Elevated blood pressure; 6MWT not performed",
  "pat was in rehabilitation therapy."   = "Patient in rehabilitation",
  "no 6mwt were done due to knee pain"   = "Knee pain; 6MWT not performed",
  "cancelled due to illness."            = "Illness; visit cancelled",
  "pat was ill"                          = "Patient ill",
  "the both 6 mwt were not performed because the patient had increased dyspnea" = "Increased dyspnoea; 6MWT not performed",
  "the both 6mwt were not performed because the patient had increased dyspnea"  = "Increased dyspnoea; 6MWT not performed",
  "pis decision - no 6 mwts were performed because the blood pressure measured at rest was too high" = "Elevated resting blood pressure (PI decision); 6MWT not performed",
  "patient was too exhausted, patient refused the 6mwt" = "Patient exhausted; refused 6MWT",
  "both 6mwt were not done due to stroke in august 2025, investigator decision" = "Stroke (Aug 2025); 6MWT not performed",
  "6mw-test was not performed, because the patient had limited mobility due to a fall. this visit is the last visit - study completed" = "Limited mobility due to fall; 6MWT not performed",
  "visit was performed by telephone, patient could not come to our site" = "Telephone visit; patient unable to attend site",
  "both 6mwt were not done due to no physicians were on the site" = "No physician on site; 6MWT not performed"
)

apply_note_lookup <- function(note) {
  key <- tolower(trimws(gsub("[[:space:]]+", " ", note)))
  if (key %in% names(note_clean_lookup)) note_clean_lookup[[key]] else note
}

for (r in reason_order) {
  sub <- miss_combos |> filter(reason == r) |> arrange(userId, visit)
  if (nrow(sub) == 0) next
  cat("\n\n**", r, "** — ",
      sum(miss_cells$reason == r), " missing cells across ",
      nrow(sub), " participant-visits.\n\n", sep = "")

  detail <- sub |>
    rowwise() |>
    mutate(
      vars_affected = {
        rows <- miss_cells[miss_cells$userId == userId & miss_cells$visit == visit, ]
        paste(unique(var_shortname(rows$variable)), collapse = ", ")
      },
      note_clean = apply_note_lookup(sanitize_note(note)),
      note_display = if (nchar(note_clean) == 0) "(blank)"
        else if (nchar(note_clean) > 180) paste0(substr(note_clean, 1, 180), "…")
        else note_clean
    ) |>
    ungroup() |>
    transmute(ID = userId, Visit = visit,
              `Vars missing` = vars_affected,
              Note = note_display)

  if (nrow(detail) > 1) {
    repeats <- duplicated(detail$ID)
    detail$ID[repeats] <- ""
  }

  print(kable(detail))
}
```

# Data quality findings — items for PRI review

The following items were identified during the data review and require action or confirmation from PRI before the D5.4 resubmission is finalised.

## Participants with no documentation of study ending

Three participants ended at T4 with complete visit data but no visit note explaining why T5 was not attended. Their T5 visits were due between July 2025 and February 2026, all of which preceded this export (April 2026). PRI is asked to confirm, for each participant, whether this was an undocumented withdrawal, a loss to follow-up, or an administrative gap.

| Participant | T4 date | T5 expected | Status |
|-------------|---------|-------------|--------|
| 21010 | 2025-04-15 | ~2025-07-15 | No documentation |
| 21029 | 2025-10-24 | ~2026-01-23 | No documentation |
| 21030 | 2025-11-17 | ~2026-02-16 | No documentation |


# Baseline characteristics

The cohort reflects a moderate-to-severe COPD population: mean FEV1% predicted of 49%, with all participants classified as GOLD grade 2 or above (21 GOLD 2, 22 GOLD 3, 3 GOLD 4) and none below 30% predicted at baseline. Mean age is 66 years with a slight male predominance (57%). This distribution is consistent with the CSA inclusion criteria and provides an appropriate disease-severity range for evaluating the CSB monitoring platform.

```{r baseline-table}
baseline <- visits$T1 |>
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(descriptives1_yob) |>
      (\(y) year(as.Date(T1Date)) - y)(),
    bmi = round(as.numeric(descriptives2_weight) /
                  ((as.numeric(descriptives1_height) / 100) ^ 2), 1),
    sex = case_when(descriptives1_sex == "1" ~ "Male",
                    descriptives1_sex == "2" ~ "Female",
                    TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    fev1_pred = as.numeric(spirometry_fev1Pred),
    fvc_pred  = as.numeric(spirometry_fvcPred),
    fev1_fvc  = as.numeric(spirometry_lungRatio),
    mmrc = as.numeric(mmrc_SHTBTH1L),
    cat  = as.numeric(cat_CATSCR1N),
    sixMWD = as.numeric(sixMinWT1_6mwd),
    ccq_total = rowMeans(across(c(ccq_breath, ccq_physical, ccq_cold, ccq_depressed,
                                  ccq_cough, ccq_phlegm, ccq_strenouspa, ccq_moderatepa,
                                  ccq_daily, ccq_social), as.numeric), na.rm = FALSE),
    mod_exac = if_else(as.numeric(exacerbate12_xModerate12) > 0, ">= 1", "0"),
    sev_exac = if_else(as.numeric(exacerbate12_xSevere12)   > 0, ">= 1", "0"),
    gold = case_when(
      fev1_pred >= 80 ~ "GOLD 1 (>=80%)",
      fev1_pred >= 50 ~ "GOLD 2 (50-79%)",
      fev1_pred >= 30 ~ "GOLD 3 (30-49%)",
      fev1_pred <  30 ~ "GOLD 4 (<30%)"
    )
  ) |>
  select(age, sex, bmi, fev1_pred, fvc_pred, fev1_fvc, gold,
         mmrc, cat, ccq_total, mod_exac, sev_exac, sixMWD)

baseline |>
  tbl_summary(
    label = list(
      age ~ "Age (years)",
      sex ~ "Sex",
      bmi ~ "BMI (kg/m²)",
      fev1_pred ~ "FEV1 (% predicted)",
      fvc_pred  ~ "FVC (% predicted)",
      fev1_fvc  ~ "FEV1/FVC ratio",
      gold      ~ "GOLD grade",
      mmrc      ~ "mMRC",
      cat       ~ "CAT score",
      ccq_total ~ "CCQ total (mean item score)",
      mod_exac  ~ "Moderate exacerbations, prior year",
      sev_exac  ~ "Severe exacerbations, prior year",
      sixMWD    ~ "6-min walk distance (m)"
    ),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ± {sd}",
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)",
      c(cat, ccq_total, mmrc) ~ "{median} ({p25} – {p75})"
    ),
    digits = all_continuous() ~ 1,
    type   = list(mmrc ~ "continuous"),
    missing = "ifany",
    missing_text = "Missing"
  ) |>
  modify_header(label ~ "**Characteristic**") |>
  bold_labels()
```

---

*Report generated `r format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")` from `data.zip` (export `r format(file.info("data.zip")$mtime, "%Y-%m-%d")`).*
